LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER CALIFORNIA MEDICAL ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. HEARING HELD AND ARGUED. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: THE PETITION HAS STANDING. THE WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. THE COURT CITES GOV. CODE SECTION 19851. (ALSO, THE COURT SUSTAINS OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE AS FOLLOWS: SPECIFIC OBJECT. TO TAN DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED; ENTIRE HINSHAW DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED; OBJECTIONS 1,2, & 3 TO CHOONG DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED; OBJECTIONS 1, 2, 3, 4, 7, 8, & 9 TO YAROSLAVSY'S DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED.) calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER CALIFORNIA MEDICAL ASSOCIATION Motion for Writ of Mandate. Appearances: Long X. Do, Esq. and Lisa Matsubara, Esq. of CMA, (916) 444-5532 appearing for Petitioner and Moving party; Tamar Pachter, Deputy Attorney General, State of California, (415) 703-5970, appearing for Respondent State Controller; Jenny L. Esquivel Esq., Department of Personnel Adm. Appearing for Respondent Schwarzenegger; David W. Tyra, Esq. of Kronick Moskovitz Tiedemann & Girard, (916) 321-4500 appearing for Respondent Schwarzenegger/Endsley; Andrew W. Stroud, Esq. of Mennemeier, Glassman & Stroud LLP (916) 551-2590 appearing for Respondent Director Michael Gewest. Hearing held and argued. The Court rules as follows: The Petition has standing. The Writ of mandate is denied. The Court cites Gov. Code section 19851. (Also, The Court sustains objections to Evidence as follows: Specific object. to Tan Declaration is sustained; entire Hinshaw Declaration is sustained; Objections 1,2, & 3 to Choong Declaration is sustained; objections 1, 2, 3, 4, 7, 8, & 9 to Yaroslavsy's Declaration is sustained.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Feb-24-2010 Continued To Mar-04-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER CALIFORNIA MEDICAL ASSOCIATION Motion for Writ of Mandate. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for writ of mandate continued to March 4, 2010 on the court's own motion. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
